education.summary: Rales Fellow: Highly selective STEM leadership program focused on mentorship, professional development, and industry engagement.

education.area: Mathematical Sciences (Statistics), Minor in Creative Writing
courses: Parallel and Sequential Data Structures and Algorithms

work.name: RedZone Robotics
highlights: Built Python/OpenCV pipeline to digitize and filter laser imagery for camera calibration
Designed custom optimization objectives and algorithms for autonomous fisheye camera parameter estimation

work.name: Mathematical Sciences Department, CMU
highlights: Led recitations for 30 students, hosted office hours, and developed course materials using LaTeX

projects.name: CMU Corporate Capstone for United States Tennis Association (USTA)
highlights: Engineered automated ETL pipelines to scrape and clean 4M+ player, tournament, and match data points
Built interactive Tableau dashboard to meet stakeholder needs, visualizing trends and predictive model insights

projects.name: AI Research Project (Supervised by Prof. Cosma Shalizi)
projects.description: Academic AI Research Project at Carnegie Mellon University
highlights: Research on sequential probability assignment for stochastic text generation
Training on HPC cluster using the DOLMA corpus (3-trillion-token dataset)

projects.name: Applied Linear Models Final Project: HIV Treatment Report
projects.description: Academic Project at Carnegie Mellon University
highlights: Analyzed clinical trial data using linear models to evaluate HIV therapy effectiveness
Found baseline CD4 count and prior therapy duration predict short-term response

projects.name: Data Engineering Final Project: Automated Data Pipeline and Dashboard
projects.description: Academic Project at Carnegie Mellon University
highlights: Designed PostgreSQL schema and ETL pipeline for large education datasets (College Scorecard, IPEDS)
Built interactive dashboard for data querying and visualization

projects.name: Statistical Principles of Generative AI Final Project
projects.description: Academic Project at Carnegie Mellon University
highlights: Generated cop-win graph dataset and applied GraphRNN to synthesize graphs
Collected and analyzed model performance data to assess accuracy

projects.name: Applied Linear Models Midterm Project: Rail Trails Report
projects.description: Academic Project at Carnegie Mellon University
highlights: Investigated impact of proximity to rail trails on home values, finding no significant effect

projects.name: Data Visualization Final Project: Dominance of the Big 3/4 in Men's Tennis
projects.description: Academic Project at Carnegie Mellon University
highlights: Cleaned and analyzed ATP Tennis data in R to visualize Big 3/4 dominance in men's professional tennis

projects.name: Software Construction Final Project: Santorini
projects.description: Academic Project at Carnegie Mellon University
highlights: Designed and built a full-stack web implementation of Santorini using Java backend and React frontend
Implemented RESTful APIs to manage game state, enforce rules, validate moves, and synchronize UI updates

projects.name: Text Analysis Final Project: Exploring Linguistic Differences Across Generations
projects.description: Academic Project at Carnegie Mellon University
highlights: Scraped, cleaned, and tokenized thousands of Reddit comments in Python to build a generational dataset
Analyzed Gen Alpha, Gen Z, and Millennial text, uncovering shifts in slang, sentiment, and topic use

projects.name: Operations Research II Final Project: Feeding CMU Orientation
projects.description: Academic Project at Carnegie Mellon University
highlights: Optimized a 900-person meal plan by scraping 1,000+ products from Trader Joe's and Whole Foods
Developed and solved an integer program to minimize costs and meet dietary constraints

projects.name: Graph Theory Research (Supervised by Prof. David Offner)
projects.description: Academic Research Project at Carnegie Mellon University
highlights: Developed mathematical tool based on the graph theory game of 'cops and robbers'
Designed graph editor, JSON converter/parser, and computer opponents with optimal strategies
